item.title: The 19th-century guide to running an effective meeting
item.description: A US officer and engineer devised the process that is still in use today

item.title: Counting the cost of New York’s sky-high food bills
item.description: Spiralling prices could lead to more public programmes

item.title: Two LNG tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Another vessel carrying crude oil crossed the vital shipping lane on Saturday

item.title: Musk, SpaceX and the world’s most far-out IPO
item.description: His ambition is to privatise the heavens — and to become the world’s first trillionaire

item.title: Italian bank CDP to raise stake in payments group Nexi after CVC weighs bid
item.description: Cassa Depositi e Prestiti’s move comes as buyout firm had considered €9bn bid for Milan-listed group

item.title: Pope Leo warns AI revolution driven by ‘idolatry of profit’
item.description: Pontiff says it is ‘not permissible’ to entrust lethal decisions to artificial systems

item.title: Prize draw businesses have better luck than the average punter
item.description: Lack of regulation has helped draw operators to thrive

item.title: AI guardrails stripped from Meta and Google models in minutes
item.description: Software designed to remove safety protections creates systems that provide responses on biological weapons and malware

item.title: Singapore tells banks to speed up account openings for wealthy clients
item.description: Regulator tries to strengthen city-state’s position as wealth hub after money-laundering cases lengthened wait times

item.title: Inside Chris Hohn’s Top Hedge Fund
item.description: Plus, James Anderson sees end to tech boom, SpaceX IPO trading frenzy and the Old Vic’s all-female Glengarry Glen Ross

item.title: Jardines signals pivot with $2.4bn Australian radiology deal
item.description: Acquisition of I-MED is Hong Kong-based conglomerate’s first since strategic overhaul

item.title: HS2 paid consultancies £65mn last year in run-up to project ‘reset’
item.description: Higher spending likely to raise questions about taxpayer-funded body’s approach to long-delayed railway

item.title: US consumers face looming spending squeeze as Trump tax rebates fade
item.description: Pressure is mounting with fuel prices driven 50% higher by Strait of Hormuz blockade

item.title: Bank lending to UK businesses falls to lowest level in nearly 30 years
item.description: Weak economic growth and tighter regulation hit credit for small and medium-sized companies

item.title: It’s not just SpaceX: Big Tech is dominating bond markets too
item.description: US tech giants are tapping markets as they race to build AI data centres

item.title: Say hello to the invisible hob
item.description: And other great new tech for the kitchen

item.title: Can Exxon build the world’s biggest carbon capture business?
item.description: Oil major bets billions on technology but backlash on US Gulf Coast threatens its ambitions

item.title: Europe’s dealmakers warn of EU merger policy confusion
item.description: Mixed messages from top EU officials cloud promised overhaul of competition rules

item.title: Tech giants need oversight to protect national security
item.description: Companies such as Anthropic and SpaceX need a presidentially-nominated, Senate-confirmed director on their boards

item.title: Fertiliser groups cut production as Iran war squeezes sulphur supplies
item.description: Farmers in poorer countries risk lower yields as they reduce how much phosphate they apply to crops

item.title: AI fever and off-exchange boom grip Japanese retail traders
item.description: Average daily trading volume on Tokyo Stock Exchange’s prime market almost doubles in 12 months

item.title: FirstFT: Xi Jinping lambasted Japan’s ‘remilitarisation’ at Donald Trump summit
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump says US will not rush deal with Iran, and Russia pounds Ukraine with hypersonic ‘Oreshnik’ missile

item.title: Uber weighs higher bid for Delivery Hero after €11.5bn offer rebuffed
item.description: San Francisco based-group approached major shareholder in German food group

item.title: West Ham relegated from Premier League
item.description: Tottenham survive as east London club ends 14-year spell in top flight of English game

item.title: Robotaxis need to be tested in real traffic
item.description: To achieve safe, cost-effective autonomy we need to see how other road users react to the vehicles

item.title: ECB summons banks to urge them to fix flaws exposed by latest AI models
item.description: Supervisor to stress seriousness of risks to financial system at hastily arranged meeting

item.title: Financial reforms set to give £1.6bn boost to City of London
item.description: Government assessment comes as Rachel Reeves champions district as driver of economic growth

item.title: Short sellers reap more than $2.3bn profit betting against gambling companies
item.description: Online groups under pressure from jump in prediction markets’ popularity in US and steep tax rises in UK

item.title: Centerbridge in talks to buy stake in $3bn real estate firm
item.description: Potential investment in Merritt Properties comes amid a busy stretch for acquisitions in commercial property

item.title: Why IPO mania could signal top of the market
item.description: A potential deluge of AI-related equity supply removes a source of upthrust for prices
